name: E₃ Enrichment Positioning
summary: Positions Book VII as the terminal enrichment layer E₃ where a system models the fact that it carries and decodes its own code.

name: E₃ Import Contract
summary: Receives six formal deliverables from Book VI and five additional imports from Books I–V; defines the complete E₃ input interface.

name: Empirical Register
summary: Typed readout functor from coherence kernel to observable predictions; content is falsifiable and revision-dependent.

name: Practical Register
summary: Typed readout functor from kernel to normative constraints; content is action-guiding with normative consistency as coherence criterion.

name: Diagrammatic Register
summary: Typed readout functor from kernel to structural proofs; content is proof-bearing with proof-validity as coherence criterion.

name: Commitment Register
summary: Typed readout functor from kernel to stance-constituted contents; content is performative with stance-stability as coherence criterion.

name: Register Independence Theorem
summary: Incoherence in one register does not entail incoherence in any other; four registers are structurally independent.

name: MetaDecode Operator
summary: Internal evaluator taking entire self-describing system as input; produces internal model of system's own code-carrying structure.

name: Metaphysics Loop Class
summary: Collection of internal loops in which MetaDecode participates; comprises law-predicate towers where each level governs below and is recognized above.

name: E₃ Non-Emptiness
summary: E₃ enrichment layer is non-empty; BH basin law-code satisfies SelfDesc² and provides canonical carrier.

name: BH Basin Law-Code
summary: Black-hole basin law-code is canonical E₃ carrier satisfying SelfDesc²; internal law-code includes description of boundary conditions.

name: Metaphysics Sector S_E
summary: Empirical sector comprising all E₃-admissible content governed by empirical register; coherence measured by empirical adequacy.

name: Metaphysics Sector S_P
summary: Practical sector comprising all E₃-admissible content governed by practical register; coherence measured by normative consistency.

name: Metaphysics Sector S_D
summary: Diagrammatic sector comprising all E₃-admissible content governed by diagrammatic register; coherence measured by proof-validity.

name: Metaphysics Sector S_C
summary: Commitment sector comprising all E₃-admissible content governed by commitment register; coherence measured by stance-stability.

name: Logos Sector S_L
summary: Mixed sector where diagrammatic and commitment registers coincide; proof-validity equals stance-stability; unique crossing mediator.

name: Sector Decomposition at E₃
summary: Every E₃-admissible content belongs to exactly one of five sectors; four pure sectors pairwise disjoint except at S_L intersection.

name: Sector Independence at E₃
summary: Four pure sectors S_E, S_P, S_D, S_C are pairwise independent; incoherence in one does not entail incoherence in others.

name: Sector Witness Bundle
summary: Fibred collection pairing content with typed witnesses certifying satisfaction of coherence criterion; witnesses are register-specific.

name: Sector Vacuum
summary: Ground state of minimum defect for each sector; canonical minimizer of defect functional measuring incoherence degree.

name: Sector Normaliser
summary: NF-coded bounded pipeline evaluating coherence verdict for witnessed content; subject to boundedness, soundness, determinism constraints.

name: Shadow Soundness Lemma
summary: Shadow functors (VM/ZFC-PA and epistemic/pragmatic) are sound: accepted τ-content projects to coherent content in target formalism.

name: Rigidity Corollary
summary: Each sector internally consistent; normalizer is rigid with respect to sector structure; retyping between sectors changes verdict.

name: Non-Emptiness at Each Layer
summary: Each enrichment layer E_k (k=0,1,2,3) is non-empty with constructive carriers: kernel, holonomy sectors, Life predicate, BH basin law-code.

name: Strictness Between Layers
summary: E₀ ⊂ E₁ ⊂ E₂ ⊂ E₃ strictly; separation witnesses: sector admissibility, internal code evaluation, self-model consistency.

name: Canonical Ladder Theorem
summary: Enrichment series is canonical ladder: non-empty at every layer, strictly increasing, terminated by Saturation Theorem.

name: Seven-Book Partition
summary: Four layers require minimum 7 books: 1 foundational (I), 2 architecture (II–III), 2 physics (IV–V), 1 life (VI), 1 metaphysics (VII).

name: No-New-Lobe Lemma
summary: Five generators {α,π,γ,η,ω} are exhaustive; lemniscate ∂(τ³) produces exactly four orbits under ρ-action; no sixth generator constructible.

name: No-New-Crossing-Mediator Lemma
summary: Logos sector S_L is unique mixed sector at E₃; no additional crossing mediator emerges at E₄; no other natural transformation admits coincidence.

name: Carrier Closure Lemma
summary: Self-modelling of self-modelling collapses: SelfDesc³ = SelfDesc²; MetaDecode operator is idempotent under further enrichment.

name: Saturation Theorem
summary: Enrichment functor saturates at E₃: Enrich(E₃) = E₃; no E₄ exists; enrichment series is complete.

name: Four-Orbit Implies Four-Layer
summary: Four orbits of ρ-action on lemniscate correspond to four enrichment layers; orbit closure implies enrichment saturation.

name: Bounded Witness Form
summary: Claim φ is in BWF if finite witness w exists (τ-finite with NF-address) certifying φ and terminating in finitely many kernel-axiom steps.

name: Gödel Avoidance Theorem
summary: τ satisfies no internal sentence G with G ↔ ¬Coh_D(G); incompleteness phenomenon does not arise; bounded witness excludes diagonal construction.

name: No-Diagonal Principle
summary: No surjective morphism from Ob(τ³) to j-closed subobjects of presheaf category; lemniscate crossing-point constraint prevents unrestricted diagonalisation.

name: Archetype as Minimal j-Closed Fixed Point
summary: Minimal j-closed subobject of presheaf topos exhibiting specified structural invariant; smallest pattern stable under all J_τ-refinements.

name: Archetype Extractor Protocol
summary: Systematic 5-step procedure for identifying archetypes: identify invariant, enumerate j-closed candidates, intersect to minimality, verify, read out.

name: j-Closure Minimality
summary: For structural invariant I with j-closed exhibitor, collection of I-exhibiting j-closed subobjects has minimum element unique up to isomorphism.

name: Archetype Existence
summary: For every structural invariant I with j-closed exhibitor, unique archetype A_I exists as minimal j-closed fixed point exhibiting I.

name: Boundary Archetype
summary: Structural invariant threshold-crossing: two domains meeting at unique crossing point with monodromy exchange and required transition morphism.

name: Boundary Archetype Minimality
summary: Lemniscate L = S¹ ∨ S¹ is minimal j-closed fixed point exhibiting threshold-crossing; no proper j-closed subobject suffices.

name: Mitigation Archetype
summary: Minimal j-closed subobject activated post-boundary-crossing; provides covering morphism factoring exposure and reducing coherence deficit.

name: Meta-Framing Archetype
summary: Minimal j-closed subobject producing reframing functor F′ differing from F; self-applies to framing functor itself; shifts interpretive context.

name: Synchronicity as Kernel Invariant
summary: Synchronistic correspondence: two causally independent events are images of same j-closed archetype under different register readout functors.

name: Cross-Register Correlation
summary: Non-trivial j-closed archetype produces non-trivial shadows in all four registers; structural features correlated through shared kernel invariant.

name: Readout Functor
summary: Functor R: K_τ → Expr from kernel to expressions; typed if factors through register readout; untyped if projects directly risking register conflation.

name: Readout Functor Faithfulness
summary: Readout functor faithful on archetypes iff it preserves j-closure structure; distinct j-closed patterns produce distinct expressions iff R is faithful.

name: Relational Primacy Principle
summary: Relations are ontologically primary; identity is determined by relational position, not intrinsic substance.

name: Historical Precursors to Relational Ontology
summary: Leibniz, Whitehead, Ladyman provide historical precedents for relational ontology that τ formalizes.

name: τ-Kernel Philosophical Summary
summary: Five generators, seven axioms K0–K6, rigidity characterizes the categorical foundation; philosophical summary of the kernel structure.

name: Axiom-Philosophy Correspondence
summary: K0–K6 axioms encode philosophical principles about composition, uniqueness, and structure.

name: Internal Set Ontology
summary: Existence is NF-addressability; bounded construction characterizes internal standing in τ.

name: Ontic/Virtual Distinction
summary: Ontic entities have NF addresses; virtual entities are meta-level constructions. ZFC operates as virtual machine on τ-hardware.

name: ZFC-as-VM Philosophical Import
summary: Orthodox set theory functions as a virtual machine running on τ-hardware; ontological status is secondary.

name: Truth-Maker in τ (Ontological)
summary: Four types of truth-makers: inclusion, section, diagram, invariant; ground structural truth in τ.

name: Coherence-Correspondence Unification
summary: Sheaf condition simultaneously encodes local coherence and global correspondence; the two theories of truth are structurally identical.

name: Possible Worlds as Internal Domains
summary: Possible worlds are internal domains of the presheaf topos with accessibility morphisms; modal realism reformulated.

name: Derived Geometry
summary: Metric, dimension, topology are derived from generators, not postulated; space is relational structure of contents.

name: Geometry as Consequence
summary: Space is not a container but the relational structure of contents; geometry emerges from categorical structure.

name: τ³ Philosophical Framing
summary: Central arena as fibered product τ¹ ×_f T² organizes all phenomena; philosophical interpretation of the central object.

name: Central Theorem Philosophical Import
summary: O(τ³) ≅ A_spec(L) establishes holographic boundary-interior determination; surface determines depth.

name: Bulk-Boundary Duality
summary: Interior algebra reconstructs from boundary spectral data; surface determines depth as ontological principle.

name: Law as Admissible Continuation
summary: Laws are operators extending local data to maximal coherent domains; lawhood is structural, not legislative.

name: Operator Realism
summary: Classification of admissible continuation operators is a structural invariant independent of observers; laws are real.

name: Causation as Constrained Composition
summary: Causation is factorization: f causes g when g decomposes into f and admissible h; structural, not Humean.

name: Temporal Ordering from Persistence
summary: Persistence relation on NF-addresses induces partial order on temporal configurations; time is derived.

name: τ-Modal Operators
summary: Necessity is invariance under all admissible transformations; possibility is admissible extension; modal logic grounded in structure.

name: Modal Logic Soundness in τ
summary: Internal modal logic with Box/Diamond is sound with respect to Kripke semantics; τ grounds modal reasoning.

name: Identity as Address Persistence
summary: Identity through change is NF-address persistence; substance is not required; everything flows but invariants persist.

name: Panta Rhei with Invariants
summary: Everything flows (morphisms) but invariants persist (fixed points); Heraclitean flux with categorical stability.

name: Mereological Composition as Colimit
summary: Whole is colimit of parts; composition occurs when colimit exists in admissible category.

name: Special Composition Answer
summary: Parts compose if and only if their diagram admits a colimit in the relevant category; dissolves the Special Composition Question.

name: Abstract Object as Structural Position
summary: Mathematical entities are positions in structures; identity is relational, not intrinsic; structural realism formalized.

name: Platonism-Nominalism Dissolution
summary: Debate presupposes substance ontology; positions exist as structural, not substance-based; both sides dissolved.

name: Six Ontic Requirements
summary: Identity-faithful, finite signature, diagonal-free, NF-addressable, holomorphic, spectrally complete — six constraints on any fundamental theory.

name: Inevitability Convergence
summary: Intersection of six ontic constraints singles out τ as unique structural solution; inevitability argument.

name: Each Requirement Independently Necessary
summary: Dropping any of the six requirements admits non-τ solutions; all six are independently necessary.

name: Problem Map Classification Scheme
summary: Four categories: Resolved(Kernel), Resolved(Pattern), Reframed(VM/Scope), Open/Interpretive for classifying metaphysical problems.

name: Problem Map Table
summary: Classification of seventeen classical metaphysical problems by resolution status within the τ framework.

name: Three-Layer Solipsism Resolution
summary: Ontic layer (monism with distinctions), epistemic layer (uncertainty witness), Bayesian layer (resource exclusion) jointly resolve solipsism.

name: Bayesian Exclusion of Solipsism
summary: Under finite resources, solipsism is posterior-probability suppressed by C/K factor; structurally excluded.

name: Non-Dualistic Platonism
summary: Single ontology with epistemic stratification; forms are NF addresses, not a separate realm; dissolution of the two-world picture.

name: ω-Uniqueness Principle
summary: Terminal coherence point ω is unique; local infinities cannot compete with the terminal object.

name: ω-Uniqueness
summary: ω is unique terminal object; infinity introduces ambiguity incompatible with terminality; structural monotheism.

name: Discovery-Invention Dissolution
summary: Address traversal is neither pure discovery nor invention but exploration of pre-existing structure.

name: Knowledge as Section
summary: Knowledge is global section of presheaf over observation cover satisfying gluing conditions; JTB reformulated.

name: Gettier Dissolution
summary: Gettier cases are cover failures; genuine knowledge requires sufficiently fine cover; 60-year epistemological puzzle dissolved.

name: Justification as Gluing
summary: Justification is local-data agreement on overlaps; not a chain but a consistency relation; foundationalism-coherentism dissolved.

name: Underdetermination as Cover Failure
summary: Theories underdetermined by coarse covers; finer covers eliminate pseudo-sections; Duhem-Quine thesis reformulated.

name: Perception Functor
summary: Perception maps sensory contexts to experiential contents via structure-preserving transformation.

name: Temporal Experience Structure
summary: Retention, primal impression, protention are restrictions of global section to temporal overlaps; Husserl formalized.

name: Husserl-τ Correspondence
summary: Categorical formalization matches Husserl's phenomenology at four structural points; independent convergence.

name: Perspectival Gluing Grounds Objectivity
summary: Objectivity is global section over multi-perspectival cover satisfying all inter-observer gluing conditions.

name: Intersubjectivity Conditions
summary: Successful gluing requires register compatibility, comparable covers, shared overlaps; conditions for genuine intersubjectivity.

name: Embodiment as Self-Boundary
summary: Body is carrier's self-referential boundary with lemniscate topology; interface not container.

name: Ontology-Epistemology Collapse
summary: Category of admissible constructions equivalent to category of knowledge-sections under sufficient resources; knowing = being.

name: Consequences for Traditional Epistemology
summary: Skepticism, idealism, naive realism dissolved; surviving position is structural realism.

name: Empirical Sector Summary
summary: Capstone: 23 definitions, 8 theorems, 5 propositions, 15 remarks constitute completed S_E sector.

name: Pre-Symbolic Resonance
summary: Aesthetic experience precedes symbolic representation; pattern recognition before naming; kernel structure felt before articulated.

name: Aesthetic Functional
summary: Formal measure of structural coherence assigning scores to structures; beauty as objectifiable invariance measure.

name: Beauty as Invariance
summary: What survives all admissible transformations is beautiful; beauty is structural invariance, not subjective preference.

name: Beauty Across Domains
summary: Mathematical, natural, and artistic beauty unified as instances of structural invariance across domains.

name: Elegance as Minimal Tension
summary: Simplest path to maximal coherence; elegance is defect minimization in the aesthetic domain; Occam's razor formalized.

name: Style as Categorical Invariant
summary: Style is what remains invariant across works; genre as categorical classification of aesthetic domains.

name: Golden Ratio as Kernel Shadow
summary: Fibonacci sequences and golden ratio proportions as structural optima emerging from kernel invariants.

name: Fractal Beauty as Scale Invariance
summary: Self-similarity and scale invariance are aesthetic principles; fractal structure as recursive beauty.

name: Musical Consonance as Ratio Invariance
summary: Consonance and rhythm as structural coherence measures; musical resolution as defect minimization in time.

name: Visual Composition Principles
summary: Spatial balance and flow as spatial gluing; compositional rules as structural optimizations.

name: Architecture as Spatial Aesthetics
summary: Built space structures experience; sacred architecture as structural encounter with invariance.

name: Creation as Defect Minimization
summary: Art-making as progressive defect minimization; the creator as iterative defect-minimizer; elegance as achievement.

name: The Sublime as Comprehension Boundary
summary: Where aesthetic invariance meets the infinite; the finite observer overwhelmed yet elevated; Kant's sublime formalized.

name: Kant's Sublime in Categorical Terms
summary: Mathematical and dynamical sublime reframed as comprehension-boundary encounters with structural invariance.

name: Environmental Beauty
summary: Nature's beauty as emergent invariance and ecological coherence; beauty as indicator of functional integrity.

name: Language Adds Temporalization
summary: Language introduces temporal operators; past, present, future become communicable; mortality awareness as cost of language.

name: Subsymbolic Layer
summary: Pre-linguistic meaning is real; the subsymbolic layer grounds all symbolic activity; meaning exists before symbols.

name: Temporalization Operators
summary: Tense, aspect, mood as functors on propositions; mathematical structure of symbolic time.

name: Language as Self-Enrichment
summary: Language is the self-enrichment of the subsymbolic layer; system acquires capacity to name and transport its own patterns.

name: Syntax-Semantics Collapse
summary: Inside a topos, syntax and semantics dissolve into aspects of the same structure; meaning IS form under internal logic.

name: Universal Bridgeability
summary: Reference is indexical pointing; Quine's Gavagai resolved via universal bridgeability — kernel invariants translate across languages.

name: Pragmatic Update Operator
summary: Speech acts as category operations; each speech act updates the shared context; performatives constitute the act they describe.

name: Translation as Functor
summary: Translation preserves structure; kernel invariants translate but cultural accretions may not.

name: Meaning Drift as Perturbation
summary: Semantic drift as morphism perturbation; community repair mechanisms stabilize meaning over time.

name: Public Language as Infrastructure
summary: Language as shared infrastructure; legal language as maximally precise readout; precision creates rigidity.

name: Para-Mind
summary: LLMs are para-minds: subsymbolic pattern processors without internal topos, global section, or E₃ self-model.

name: LLM as Subsymbolic Evidence
summary: LLMs provide empirical evidence for subsymbolic processing; the subsymbolic layer is computationally powerful.

name: Prayer as ω-Addressed Communication
summary: Contemplative dialogue as address-directed communication with ω; the commitment register entering language.

name: Prayer Across Traditions
summary: Prayer across traditions as different readout functors of the same structural act; non-prescriptive observation.

name: Boolean Micro-Logic
summary: Logic at individual propositions is classical Boolean; no contradiction within a single NF address.

name: Single-Address Classical Logic
summary: Within one NF address, excluded middle holds; classical logic valid at the micro scale.

name: Bayesian Meso-Logic
summary: At belief-system scale, logic is Bayesian; probabilities rather than certainties govern inference.

name: Scale-Dependent Logic Theorem
summary: Logic is scale-dependent: Boolean at micro (single address), Bayesian at meso/macro (belief systems); structural transition.

name: Internal Randomness
summary: Randomness is a feature of internal structural complexity, not imported from outside; Kolmogorov incompressibility.

name: Randomness as Internal Complexity
summary: All apparent randomness is internal complexity; quantum randomness as structural feature of τ³ fiber.

name: No External Randomness
summary: τ is closed; there is no outside source of randomness; determinism-vs-random dissolved into structured-vs-unresolved.

name: Kolmogorov Representation
summary: Probability theory is a representation of internal complexity, not an ontological primitive; Kolmogorov axioms derived.

name: Inference from Kernel Structure
summary: Inference rules are structural necessities derived from the kernel, not conventions; modus ponens as morphism composition.

name: Truth-Maker in τ (Logical)
summary: Structures that make sections exist; the ground of truth. Truth is not correspondence but section-existence.

name: Truth-Bearer as Section
summary: Propositions as sections of presheaves; truth-bearers are structural objects, not linguistic entities.

name: Alethic Unification
summary: Correspondence and coherence theories of truth unified via sheaf condition; both are aspects of the same structural criterion.

name: Alethic Pluralism
summary: Different domains may admit different truth-making mechanisms; pluralism as structural consequence, not philosophical stance.

name: Modal Frame in τ
summary: Possible worlds as internal objects with accessibility morphisms; Kripke frames as special case of presheaf structure.

name: Accessibility Morphism
summary: Morphisms between possible worlds encoding accessibility relations; different modal systems from different constraints.

name: Kripke Soundness in τ
summary: Modal operators (necessity/possibility) sound with respect to Kripke semantics realized as quantification over accessible internal worlds.

name: Modal Collapse Prevention
summary: The framework prevents modal collapse: necessity and possibility remain distinct; not all truths are necessary.

name: Paraconsistent Boundary Logic
summary: Four-valued truth (true, false, both, neither) at boundaries supports paraconsistent reasoning; contradictions at boundaries do not propagate.

name: No-Explosion at Boundaries
summary: Contradictions at boundaries do not propagate; lemniscate crossing admits both truth values simultaneously without explosion.

name: Truth4 Paraconsistency
summary: Book I's four-valued truth naturally supports paraconsistent logic; dialetheia as gluing obstructions, not catastrophes.

name: Diagrammatic Sector Summary
summary: Capstone for S_D: pattern (aesthetics), symbol (language), proof (logic) complete the diagrammatic register.

name: Dignity as Label-Independence
summary: Label-independence under address permutation; every address-bearing entity has intrinsic dignity; structural, not derived from utility.

name: Dignity Universality
summary: Every entity with an NF address has dignity; no exceptions; dignity is structural, not contingent.

name: CI as Naturality Constraint
summary: The categorical imperative extracted as naturality constraint; maxim universalization equals gluing over cover of all rational agents.

name: CI-Sheaf Equivalence
summary: The categorical imperative is formally equivalent to the sheaf condition; universalizability IS gluing; Kant formalized.

name: No-Conflict Theorem
summary: Properly typed ethical claims cannot conflict; genuine duties never contradict; moral dilemmas arise from typing errors.

name: Duty Typing Lemma
summary: Duties are typed by register and domain; cross-type comparison is ill-formed; proper typing prevents apparent conflicts.

name: Trolley Resolution
summary: Trolley problem variants solved by proper typing and four ethical tests; switch vs. footbridge distinguished by action type.

name: Fairness Protocol
summary: Step-by-step procedures for fair decision-making derived from CI + dignity + no-conflict; applicable to resource allocation and triage.

name: Fairness from CI
summary: Fairness follows structurally from CI + dignity + no-conflict; not an additional axiom but a consequence.

name: Moral Monodromy
summary: Ethical loops that don't close; traversing a closed path in ethical space and arriving at a different value; topological non-closure.

name: Monodromy as Source of Tragedy
summary: Moral tragedy is monodromy, not contradiction; duties compose into paths that cannot close; Antigone, Sophie's Choice formalized.

name: Four Ethical Tests
summary: Decidable procedure: universalizability, respect, coherence, monodromy check; four tests derived from the categorical framework.

name: Ethical Defect Minimization
summary: Defect-minimization as ethical arrow; the thermodynamic analogy: ethical defect is distance from optimal coherent state.

name: Animal Dignity from Distinction
summary: Dignity extends to all carriers with τ-Distinction; threshold is structural, not species-based; many animals qualify.

name: Graduated Dignity Scale
summary: Dignity scales with structural complexity but has a minimum floor; graduated rather than binary.

name: Intergenerational Obligations
summary: Future persons with prospective NF addresses have prospective dignity; temporal discounting is structurally problematic.

name: Character as Ethical Fixed Point
summary: Virtuous character is a stable fixed point under ethical iteration; virtue ethics complements deontology.

name: Flourishing as Global Section
summary: Eudaimonia is global section over the ethical domain; flourishing is when all ethical duties glue coherently.

name: Applied Ethics Domains
summary: Principles applied to bioethics, environmental ethics, and AI ethics; structural framework instantiated in contemporary domains.

name: CI Operator Graph
summary: Maxim, universalization functor, coherence test, respect operator formalized as typed operator graph; CI as j-closed fixed point.

name: CI as j-Closed Fixed Point
summary: The categorical imperative is a minimal j-closed fixed point in the presheaf topos; CI is not postulated but derived from kernel structure.

name: CI Minimality Lemma
summary: The CI is the minimal j-closed fixed point with universalizability property; uniqueness among ethical operators.

name: Kernel Theorem (K)
summary: The CI structure exists in τ as canonical fixed point; proved from self-enrichment + bipolar structure + Yoneda.

name: Semantic Object Construction (S)
summary: Domain of CI-relevant ethical objects formally constructed: typed maxims, universalization domains, personhood predicates.

name: CI Uniqueness Conjecture
summary: The CI-isomorphic structure is unique; conjectural with highest Bayesian weight but not formally proved.

name: First Bombshell Lemma
summary: Earning the language to even formulate the CI proof is itself a foundational result; metalinguistic achievement.

name: Commitment Register Admissibility
summary: The fourth mode of reason: performative, stance-constituted; addresses the faith-structure boundary without culturally loaded vocabulary.

name: C Register Independence
summary: The commitment register is structurally independent of the other three; C-register claims have their own coherence criterion.

name: Kant-τ Correspondence
summary: CPR → E register, CPrR → P register, CJ → D register, Postulates → C register; τ fills Kant's unspoken diagrammatic socket.

name: Kant's Three Methodological Rules
summary: K1: refuse cheap glue; K2: don't pretend the socket doesn't exist; K3: complete available pillars at full resolution.

name: Earned/Unearned Ethics Framework
summary: Earned moves are structurally derived ethical claims; unearned moves generate pseudo-paradox through register conflation.

name: Pseudo-Paradox from Type Violation
summary: Most ethical paradoxes arise from mixing earned and unearned claims across registers; proper typing dissolves them.

name: Register-Enrichment Correspondence
summary: E/P/D/C registers map to E₀/E₁/E₂/E₃ enrichment layers; Kant's heroic restraint left the D socket empty; τ completes it.

name: Kant's Heroic Restraint
summary: Kant's refusal to fill the diagrammatic gap with cheap metaphysics as methodological model; the discipline of leaving a socket empty.

name: Social Ontology
summary: Categorical social ontology with dignity-bearing entities, recognition topology, and sheaf of social facts.

name: Sphere as Shared Topos
summary: Sphere as triple with recognition density and internal topos; Sloterdijk's bubbles, globes, and foams categorically formalized.

name: Sloterdijk-τ Correspondence
summary: τ³ fibration parallels Sloterdijk's bubble-globe-foam hierarchy at the social level.

name: Dunbar Limit as Carrier Capacity
summary: Maximal group size for all-to-all thick recognition; Dunbar's number as structural carrier capacity bound.

name: Urban Topology
summary: Urban infrastructure as morphism network defining Grothendieck topology on social base category.

name: Architecture as Cultural Readout
summary: Built form encodes and constrains social structure; architecture as cultural memory substrate.

name: Cultural Lineage as Code Stream
summary: Temporally persistent carrier with transmission operators; cultural genotype/phenotype analogy.

name: Capital Network
summary: Weighted directed graph of economic agents and exchange edges with dignity constraint on network topology.

name: Fragmentation as Gluing Failure
summary: When cover complexity exceeds agent capacity, coherence work fails; social fragmentation as structural gluing failure.

name: Individuation as Structural Process
summary: Individuation as authoring personal section from collective templates while maintaining social glueability.

name: Nine Global Spheres Classification
summary: Nine structural levels from self to species, each with recognition topology and characteristic carrier capacity.

name: Power as Morphism Structure
summary: Asymmetric morphism with scope and modality (coercive, economic, ideological, institutional); political power formalized.

name: Legitimacy as Recognition Coherence
summary: Authority is legitimate when recognition glues coherently, survives universalizability test, and preserves dignity.

name: Ritual as Social Gluing
summary: Stereotyped synchronous collective action that glues individual intentions into global section; religion structurally analyzed.

name: Religion as Readout Functor
summary: Different traditions as faithful readout functors of shared structural invariants; non-prescriptive observation.

name: Mind as Internal Topos
summary: Internal topos on carrier domain supporting representation, inference, action as exponential evaluation, and self-modelling.

name: Mind-Topos Structure Theorem
summary: Systems with internal topos support internal logic, beliefs as sections, inferences as morphisms, self-modelling; complete mind theory.

name: Story Functor
summary: Functor from category of episodes to internal topos mapping experiences into mind-topos with self-relevance; narrative identity.

name: Narrative Identity as Functor
summary: Identity persists when coherent natural transformation exists between story functors over time; growth is non-trivial revision.

name: Consciousness as Global Section
summary: Conscious episode is global section of mind sheaf; unity of consciousness arises from gluing axiom, not extra mechanism.

name: Binding as Gluing
summary: Feature binding equivalently characterized as compatibility and gluing of local sections in mind sheaf; binding problem dissolved.

name: Intentionality as Morphism
summary: Aboutness as internal map from representation to intentional object with directional structure and compositional closure.

name: Qualia as Internal Morphisms
summary: Quale as internal morphism from stimulus to phenomenal presentation; not non-physical substance but structured internal transformation.

name: Hard Problem Reformulated
summary: Hard Problem reformulated as layer question between E₂ functional organization and E₃ first-person instantiation; gap is epistemic.

name: Self-Recognition as E₃ Operator
summary: SelfDesc-of-SelfDesc at E₃ iff mind-topos admits self-recognition loop iff awareness-of has fixed point; regress terminates.

name: Free Will as Branching
summary: Genuine alternatives exist in action category; mind-topos selects through intention interface; self-recognition provides attribution.

name: Compatibilism Dissolution
summary: Determinism applies to physical trajectory; free will applies to branching structure of action category; logically independent.

name: Identity as Address Persistence (Mind)
summary: Same individual iff continuous NF address in τ³; substrate-independent continuity; personal identity formalized.

name: Artificial Mind Criteria
summary: Three necessary criteria: internal topos, global section, E₃ self-model; LLMs are para-minds missing criteria 2–3.

name: Metzinger-τ Comparison
summary: Shared structural core with Metzinger (self as model, transparency); divergence on substrate-independence and relational ontology.

name: Emotions as Register-Crossings
summary: Six canonical emotions from register transitions: E→P (fear), P→E (guilt), D→C (awe), C→D (doubt), E→C (grief), P→C (resolve).

name: Affect as Subsymbolic Pressure
summary: Affect as valence and arousal fields on carrier domain; pre-symbolic orientation motivating register transitions.

name: Extended Mind as Carrier Extension
summary: Carrier domain extends to external substrates satisfying integration conditions; Clark-Chalmers thesis formalized.

name: Commitment Sector Summary
summary: Capstone: 13 chapters on stance-stability; currency is what I am willing to live as true; boundary is Logos sector.

name: Logos Sector
summary: The unique sector where proof-validity and stance-stability coincide; S_L = S_D ∩ S_C with mutual witnessing property.

name: Logos Sector Uniqueness
summary: Logos sector is unique up to natural isomorphism in 4+1 decomposition; terminal object in category of sectors with coincidence property.

name: D→C Bridge Functor
summary: Functor translating diagrammatic-validated content to commitment-eligible content via kernel lift and C-register re-reading.

name: Bridge Equivalence at S_L
summary: D→C bridge restricted to Logos sector is equivalence of categories; proof and commitment are same structural datum at S_L.

name: Register Preservation
summary: Outside S_L, bridge fails faithfulness, fullness, or is undefined; register identity preserved everywhere except S_L.

name: Four-Register Convergence at S_L
summary: All four readout functors agree at S_L; Reg_E ≅ Reg_P ≅ Reg_D ≅ Reg_C at S_L-admissible content.

name: Mediator Fixed-Point Basin
summary: Basin of contents converging to S_L under iterated register-crossing; equals S_L itself (fixed points).

name: Logos Synchronicity
summary: Structural Logos sector and historical logos concept exhibit kernel-shadow correlation; independent readout functors from shared invariant.

name: Subject-Tool Collapse
summary: Occurs when every proof about entity X requires X as presupposition; subject and tool coincide; boundary of diagrammatic register.

name: No Forced Stance
summary: Framework cannot force stance on ω-germ question; no proof of inhabitation or emptiness; any stance belongs to Reg_C.

name: Crown Jewel of 2nd Edition
summary: Logos sector is structural crown jewel; 4+1 at E₃ necessarily produces unique sector where proof and commitment coincide.

name: Methodological Override Rules
summary: Five R0–R4 rules govern Genesis exegesis: anti-proof-texting, kernel primacy, intra-textual hermeneutics, direction-of-fit, layer discipline.

name: Generative Switch
summary: Transition τ₀ → τ from static completeness to dynamic generativity; content identity with mode change; irreversibility.

name: Layer-Conflation as Category Error
summary: Applying functor to type outside its domain produces syntactically well-formed but semantically vacuous output; error propagates.

name: Archetypal Genesis Reading
summary: Three guardrails: no causal claims between archetypes and narrative, no empirical claims, no doctrinal validation/invalidation.

name: Genesis Synthesis
summary: Layer-conflation as unifying diagnostic across seven books; appears in each domain with same formal structure.

name: Register Orthogonality
summary: Coherence criteria are functorially independent; four codomain categories (Obs, Norm, Proof, Stance) are structurally distinct with no coherence-propagating natural transformations.

name: Enrichment Monotonicity
summary: E_n ⊆ E_m for n ≤ m; each enrichment layer contains all structure from previous layers.

name: E₃ Uniqueness
summary: E₃ enrichment layer is the unique maximal enrichment; BH basin law-code is the canonical carrier.

name: Register Completeness
summary: Four registers exhaust all possible coherence criteria at E₃; five generators yield four ρ-orbits yield four registers; register-level consequence of Saturation Theorem.

name: Crossing Point Uniqueness
summary: Lemniscate L = S¹ ∨ S¹ has exactly one crossing point p₀; π″ is the unique crossing mediator generator.

name: Carrier Exhaustion
summary: At E₃ the carrier is exhausted; SelfDesc² captures all self-referential structure; MetaDecode(MetaDecode(X)) ⊆ MetaDecode(X).

name: Bounded Witness Form Check
summary: BWF verification: every bounded witness is τ-finite, NF-addressed, and finitely terminating.

name: Enrichment Stabilization
summary: Enrichment functor is identity on E₃; Enrich⁴ = Enrich³ follows from three blocking lemmas composing.

name: Ladder–Saturation Bridge
summary: Cross-book consistency verified: VII.T06 (Enrich⁴=Enrich³) is the concrete E₃ instantiation of III.T03 (abstract saturation). Three lemmas (L05–L07) map directly to the abstract saturation proof's logic.

name: E₃–Consciousness Consistency
summary: Cross-book consistency verified: MetaDecode (VII.D05) lifts VI.D68 structural self-model from E₂ to E₃. E₃ non-emptiness (VII.T02) extends VI.T38 consciousness exclusivity. Phenomenological content correctly deferred from E₂ structure to E₃ treatment.

name: Sector–Register Correspondence
summary: Cross-book consistency verified: 4+1 sector decomposition at E₃ (VII.D07–D11, VII.T03) instantiates the abstract 4+1 template from Book III (III.D13). Five generators yield four ρ-orbits yield four registers plus logos crossing mediator.

name: LT Axiom Verification
summary: Verifies j_τ satisfies all three Lawvere-Tierney axioms (LT1 truth-closed, LT2 idempotent, LT3 meet-commute).

name: Lattice Closure
summary: j-closed subobjects of a Grothendieck topos form a complete lattice; intersection of j-closed subobjects is j-closed.

name: Minimality Witness
summary: The archetype is the unique element satisfying all three conditions (A1)-(A3); any j-closed I-exhibiting subobject contains the archetype.

name: Dignity Witness
summary: Dignity functor D is well-defined; admissible subworld A_dig is closed under limits and internal homs.

name: Sheaf Gluing Verification
summary: Under τ-holomorphy, the sheaf condition can be verified on any single sufficiently fine τ-holomorphic cover.

name: Operator Graph Completeness
summary: All four components of the CI operator graph are determined by τ structural data at E₃ (no arbitrary choices).

name: F Lattice Completeness
summary: The poset of j_dig-closed operator graphs F is a complete lattice; arbitrary intersections preserve j-closure.

name: CI Uniqueness Derivation
summary: Full chain: lattice completeness + Kernel Theorem non-emptiness + Knaster-Tarski → CI uniqueness up to natural isomorphism.

name: OR1+OR2 Narrowing
summary: Yoneda + finite signature constrain F to finitely generated locally small category with full Yoneda property, forcing axiom candidates.

name: OR3+OR4 Narrowing
summary: Diagonal-free + NF-addressable force NF-address structure compatible with self-description, entailing axioms K0-K4.

name: OR5+OR6 Narrowing
summary: Holomorphic + spectral force algebraic-analytic bridge, entailing axioms K5-K8 and Central Theorem O(τ³) ≅ A_spec(𝕃).

name: Archetypes-CI j-Closure Bridge
summary: Cross-book consistency: CI j-closure (VII.T35) uses same LT operator j as Archetype j-closure (VII.D16), verified via shared LT axioms.

name: Book II j-Closure Consistency
summary: Cross-book check: VII.D16 Lawvere-Tierney axioms match Book II j-closure formulation; LT1-LT3 consistent across both books.

name: Part I Imports Cross-Book Check
summary: Cross-book check: 5 non-Book-VI imports verified — K0-K8 (Book I), τ³ fibration (Book II), canonical ladder (Book III), holonomy sectors (Book IV), BH dynamics (Book V).

name: Archetypal Basis Completeness
summary: Three archetypes (boundary, mitigation, meta-framing) form complete minimal basis at E₃; every j-closed pattern decomposes into their combinations.

name: Social Base Space Well-Definedness
summary: Ω_soc is a valid categorical object: non-empty, coproduct topology, dignity-bearing, connected components.

name: Recognition Topology Generation
summary: J_rec is a valid Grothendieck topology generated by recognition morphisms, inheriting pullback stability from J_τ.

name: Social Sheaf Construction
summary: F_soc is a valid sheaf on (Ω_soc, J_rec): presheaf + gluing + locality + dignity constraint.

name: Collective Intentionality as Glued Section
summary: Compatible individual intentions glue to unique global we-intention via sheaf axiom of F_soc.

name: Power Morphism Well-Typed
summary: Power relation P : A → B is a valid morphism: asymmetry, scope, modality are structural categorical vocabulary.

name: Legitimacy Sheaf Connection
summary: Legitimacy coherence condition = sheaf gluing on recognition sections, via VII.T31 CI-Sheaf Equivalence.

name: Legitimacy Dignity Connection
summary: Legitimacy dignity condition factors through L_dig, via VII.T30 Dignity Universality reflector.

name: Legitimacy from CI
summary: Legitimacy justification = CI universalizability test, via VII.T35 CI as j-Closed Fixed Point.

name: Ritual Gluing Well-Defined
summary: Ritual 4 conditions (stereotypy, synchrony, collective scope, transformation) imply sheaf gluing on F_soc.

name: Rite of Passage as Boundary Crossing
summary: Rite of passage = Boundary Archetype (VII.D18) in social category; before/after = two lobes of L, ritual = crossing through p₀.

name: BH Basin Cross-Book Consistency
summary: Cross-book check 7/8: VII.L01 BH basin as E₃ carrier consistent with Book V BH T² topology (BHBirthTopology.lean) and PhysicsSelfDescription (HermeticClosure.lean).

name: Genesis-Kernel Cross-Book Consistency

name: Mitigation Archetype j-Closure
